nodes.label: ADR-001: JAX vs NumPy Backend
nodes.author: Architecture Team
nodes.rationale: JAX adopted as primary computation backend for JIT compilation (2-10x speedup for energy sweeps), vmap replaces manual threading, automatic differentiation enables future NLSQ fitting. NumPy retained at I/O boundaries.

nodes.label: ADR-002: PyQtGraph vs Matplotlib
nodes.author: Architecture Team
nodes.rationale: PyQtGraph replaces matplotlib for GUI plotting: native Qt rendering via QPainter eliminates Agg renderer overhead, inherits Qt palette automatically, handles 10k+ points at 60fps. Matplotlib retained for publication-quality static export.

nodes.label: ADR-003: float64 Preservation Policy
nodes.author: Architecture Team
nodes.rationale: float64 is non-negotiable for X-ray calculations — intermediate products span 30+ orders of magnitude (Thompson constant ~2.8e-15 × Avogadro ~6e23 = ~2.7e14). float32 would yield ~10% relative error. JAX_ENABLE_X64 forced at startup.

nodes.label: ADR-004: Host-Device Transfer Minimization
nodes.author: Architecture Team
nodes.rationale: Minimize JAX/NumPy transfers with a 2-transfer strategy: input enters JAX at calculate_single_material_properties(), output converts back at XRayResult. Batch processing shares input transfer, reducing from 2N to 2 transfers for N materials.
